name: Sourdough Potato Bread Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in warm water and add sugar and instant potatoes. Stir until dissolved.
2. Let the mixture stand at room temperature for all day or overnight before refrigerating.
3. Feed the starter with 3/4 cup sugar, 1 cup warm water, and 3 tbsp of instant potatoes (heaping).
4. After mixing in the feed mixture, let the starter stand at room temperature for a full day or night (8-12 hours).
5. Cover with a paper towel or cloth. The starter will bubble but not rise.
6. Use one cup of the starter to make bread and return the rest to the refrigerator.
7. Keep the remaining starter in the refrigerator for 3-5 days and feed it every 3-5 days.
8. If you are not making bread, after feeding, discard 1 cup of the starter by giving or throwing it away.
